title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. vulnerarioides
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. vulnerarioides, also known as Kidney Vetch, is a plant species belonging to the legume family, Fabaceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and pink or red markings. Traditionally, Kidney Vetch has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant is rich in flavonoids and saponins, providing health benefits such as antioxidant, antimicrobial, and immunomodulatory effects, which can aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Kidney Vetch is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used as a topical application to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
